description: Multi-file generic repository + service that must SATISFY a pre-defined generic interface. Missing a method or returning the wrong type fails tsc — a classic weak-model slip the auto-verify gate catches.
prompt: Strict TypeScript project. src/types.ts already defines a generic `Repository<T>` interface and a `Product` type — read it, do not change it. (1) Create src/product-repo.ts exporting class `InMemoryProductRepository` that `implements Repository<Product>` — implement EVERY method of the interface with the exact signatures (an in-memory Map is fine). (2) Create src/product-service.ts exporting class `ProductService` whose constructor takes a `Repository<Product>` and which has `addProduct(p: Product): void`, `getProduct(id: string): Product | undefined`, and `totalInventoryValue(): number` (sum of price*stock over all products). Everything must pass `npx tsc --noEmit` with zero errors. Do not use `any` or `@ts-ignore` to silence errors — make the types actually correct.
